folio.label: 35v
folio.l: Freedom , and al that longeth , to that art
So Iubiter haue , of my soule part
As in this world rigút now , ne knowe I non
So worthy to ben loued , as Palamon
That serueth ©ow , and wol don al his lyf
And if æat euäe , ©e shuln ben a wyf´
For©ete nat Palamou~ , the gentil man
¢ And with that word , his speche faile began
For fro his fete , vp to his brest was come
The colde of deth , that had hi~ ouäcome
And ©et more ouä , for in his armes two
The vitayÂ strengthe , is lost and al a go
Oonly the intellect , with outen more
That dwelletú , in his hert , seke and sore
Gan faylen , whan the hert feleth deth
Dusked ys hise eyen two , and faileth breth
But on his lady , ©et cast he his eye
His last word , was mäcy Emelye
His spirit chaunged hens , & went there
As I cam neuäe , I kan nat tellen where
Therfore I stynt , I am no diuinistre
Of soules fynde I nou©t , in this registre
Ne me ne list , thilke opinions to telle
Of hem , though æ æei wryten , where they dwelle
Arcite is cold , there Mars his soule gye
Now wol I speken forth , of Emelye
¢ Shryghte , Emelye , and howletú Palamon~
And Theseus his Suster , tok a non~
Swoughnyng , & bar hire , fro the corps a way

note.notetxt: 1955There is a cross in dry point in the left margin.ODR

folio.l: What helpeth it , to tarien forth the day
To tellen how she wepte , bothe eue and morwe
For in swicú cas , wo~men han swicú sorwe
Whan æ here husbondes , ben fro hem a go
That for the more ¹t , they sorwen so
Or ellis fallen , in swicú a maladie
That at the last , certeynly they deye
¢ Infinite ben the sorwes , and the teeres
Of olde folk , and folk of tendre ©eres
In al theToun , for the detú , of this Theban
For hi~ æäe wepeth , bothe child & man
So gret a wepyng , was æäe non certeyn
Whan Ector was brougút , al fressú I slayn
To Troye , allas the pyte that was there
Cracchyng of chekes , rendyng eke of here
Why woldest æè be ded , theise wo~men crye
